dateline: New Delhi / Islamabad / Srinagar / United Nations — August 1947–Present
heroHeadline: Pakistan Escalates IWT Dispute to UNSC on One-Year Suspension Anniversary; Field Marshal Munir Warns India of 'Decisive Response'
heroSubtitle: On April 24, 2026 — exactly one year after India suspended the 1960 Indus Waters Treaty following the Pahalgam massacre — Pakistan's FM Ishaq Dar delivered a formal letter to the UN Security Council president demanding India restore the treaty and cease 'water weaponisation,' marking the first UNSC escalation on the IWT in the treaty's 65-year history. Simultaneously, Field Marshal Asim Munir warned that Pakistan's forces are 'fully prepared' to deliver a 'decisive response' to any Indian aggression, maintaining Pakistan's nuclear-backed deterrence posture one year after the May 2025 Operation Sindoor exchange. India made no immediate response to the UNSC letter, with officials reiterating the treaty will not be restored. The bilateral standoff at one year: airspace closure, IWT suspension, minimal diplomatic staffing, and the May 2025 ceasefire technically holding — with no normalization talks underway.
footerNote: Data sourced from Indian MEA/MOD, Pakistan ISPR/MFA, UN Security Council, SIPRI, Crisis Group, HRW, Stimson Center
